reasoning.typography: Inter Variable is competently deployed with disciplined weights. Large headline has presence, but it's the B2B SaaS default—no distinctive character beyond 'professional polish'.
reasoning.hierarchy: Hero headline dominates adequately, but secondary content ('Purpose-built for modern teams...') competes for attention mid-page. Logo grid creates visual noise that diffuses focus.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Complete stencil. Swap 'Linear' for any productivity tool and it works identically—dark background, large sans-serif promise, logo wall validation. Zero unique structural decisions.
reasoning.color: Monochrome restraint is excellent. Pure white on black with minimal accent usage. The logo wall maintains the discipline without color pollution.
reasoning.density: Vast empty hero is generic 'confident whitespace' theatre. Mid-section crowds logos and body copy together awkwardly. No distinctive rhythm—just standard spacing presets.
reasoning.voice: IA is pure stencil: hero promise → social proof logos → elaboration → features. 'Purpose-built for the AI era' could describe 47 different products. No narrative specificity.

winning_patterns: Color discipline is exceptional—pure monochrome with no decorative pollution
Typography scale and weight hierarchy are professionally executed
Logo wall credibility signaling is cleanly rendered without garish styling
anti_patterns: Completely interchangeable structure—hero/logos/description template applied without adaptation
Headline 'for teams and agents' feels AI-era keyword stuffing rather than natural voice
Massive empty hero wasteland is 'expensive confidence' cosplay, not purposeful density
Logo grid interrupts reading flow awkwardly between headline and explanation
'Purpose-built... AI workflows... new standard' is buzzword bingo, not distinctive positioning
